content.full_text:
A- 98-200

November 7, 1978

Mr. William S. Marra
Associate Counsel
Farm PAC
2855 Telegraph Avenue
Berkeley, California 94705

Dear Mr. Marra:

This letter is in response to your letter concerning the California
Farm Bureau Federation Political Action Committee's reporting of con-
tributors under Government Code Section 84210(g) of the Political
Reform Act. You specifically inquired about the necessity of the
street address for the place of business for self-employed contributors.

The Commission staff has interpreted Section 84210(g) to require that
when a principal place of business is listed for a self-employed con-
tributor the address must also be included. When reporting the
employer of a contributor, the entity must be identified by name.
The listing of the city or county is not sufficient identification
to satisfy this provision. In cases where the contributor is self-
employed it is acceptable to use the word "same" or another indication
that the employment address is the same as that of the contributor.
In your situation, FARM PAC has many contributors who reside on farms.
The listing of "farmer" as an occupation and "same" as the principal
place of business is acceptable.

The following is an example of proper listing of contributors on
Schedule A, Part 2 of Form 420.

Full Name and Address (Street,    Occupation        Employer (if contributor
City, State) of Contributor                        is self-employed list
                                                   Street Address and City
                                                   of Business)

Joseph Smith                        Farmer            Same
Rural Route 2A
Hancock, California

Timothy Jones                        Feed & Grain      Jones Feed & Grain
29 First Street                      Distributor       345 Ione Way
Hancock, California                  (Self-Employed)   Hancock, California

Mary O'Connor                        Marketing         Marshall & Associates
213 Second Avenue                    Specialist
Hancock, California

Mr. William Marra                    -2-                    November 7, 1978


If you wish to appeal this advice or you require additional information,
please do not hesitate to contact me.

Very truly yours,


Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator


cc: Gene Morrow
    Secretary of State's Office

    Robert Summers
    Franchise Tax Board


RSK:bw

To
Protect
And To
Preserve

FARM PAC®
California Farm Bureau Federation Political Action Committee (FARM PAC)
Frederick J. Heringer, Chairman          Howard P. Wackman II, Treasurer

2855 TELEGRAPH AVENUE
BERKELEY, CALIFORNIA 94705
TELEPHONE (415) 644-7107
September 26, 1978

Regina Kutchins, Technical Assistance Advisor
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804

Re: Campaign Reporting

Dear Ms. Kutchins:

I am writing this letter regarding campaign reporting requirements of Government Code §84210(g).

The California Farm Bureau Federation Political Action Committee (FARM PAC) files periodic campaign statements (Form 420).

Because FARM PAC has large numbers of contributors, it files Schedule A, Part 2 - Received from Others - on a computer print out. Accordingly, that page on the Secretary of State's form is not used.

In complying with Government Code §84210(g), FARM PAC lists the full name of each contributor of $50.00 or more, together with his street address and occupation. If he is employed, we list the name of his employer. If he is self employed, we list his principal place of business, which is either the city or the county in which the individual's principal place of business is located. We do not list the street address of the contributor's principal place of business if he is self employed. Likewise, we do not list the street address of his employer if he is employed.

FARM PAC was informed by letter from the Secretary of State that the omission of the street address of the principal place of business of the self employed individual was a failure to conform with the disclosure requirements.

It is my belief that listing the principal place of business by city or county complies with C. C. §84210 and I am unaware of any other statute or regulation which requires listing the street address of the business of the self employed individual. It is also my belief that this satisfies the intent of the disclosure requirements of the Political Reform Act. Each contributor of $50.00 or more is listed by name and address. Any further inquiries can be directed to that address. I fail to see what value the street address of the self employed person could add to the disclosure requirements.

September 26, 1978


In addition, many of FARM PAC'S contributors are farmers and ranchers in rural
areas. They have no "street address and city of business" as contemplated by
Form 420. Please be assured that FARM PAC is not trying to evade its reporting
requirements under the law. FARM PAC has instituted rigid internal control
devices to insure that all contributions received are properly accounted for.
We do object however, to filing information which is not required by the statute,
particularly since the information sought seems to have marginal value, at best.

I am available to discuss this matter with yourself or any other person at your
convenience. Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
WILLIAM S. MARRS
Associate Counsel


WSM/eih
